name: Exact residual extension tests for link orbits 8 and 9
hypothesis: One of the two newly validated point-link orbits may extend to a directly verifiable 40-block cover; otherwise replayed UNSAT will exclude each orbit locally.
expected_signal: Exactly two fixed-link results at 300 seconds each, with direct cover verification for SAT and reconstructed CNF plus replayable proof for UNSAT.
decision_value: A verified SAT settles C(12,6,4)=40; two replayed UNSAT results establish durable local nonextensions and return attention to the 12-case hard tail.

efficiency_design.naive_cost: Returning immediately to the 12-node exclusion hard tail could spend at least 720 solver-seconds without testing whether either new orbit supplies a decisive positive 40-cover.
opportunities_considered: Exact fixed-link residual extension for each of the two new orbits.
A stratified three-node nine-orbit frontier discriminator.
efficiency_design.chosen_reductions: Condition on each independently validated 20-block point link, leaving only the exact residual 20-block completion problem. Test exactly two representatives and stop immediately on SAT.
efficiency_design.expected_throughput_gain: Two conditioned residual searches directly test theorem-settling witnesses in at most 600 solver-seconds and avoid unrelated frontier or generic local-search work.
efficiency_design.soundness_basis: Both point links passed fresh direct validation and complete C2 wr S5 canonicalization. SAT is checked directly over all 495 quadruples; UNSAT requires independent CNF reconstruction and external DRAT replay before acceptance.
efficiency_design.remains_uncompressed: The point-link catalogue is still not exhaustive, and the 12 genuine timeout leaves remain open if both fixed-link extensions are excluded.
